← All examples

TCC handicap scoring (IRC / PY)

Time-on-time corrected scoring using a Time Correction Factor (TCF). IRC boats use TCC directly; PY boats derive TCF = 1000 ÷ PY number. Boats rank by lowest corrected time (CT = ET × TCF); penalty codes are unchanged.

Examples

#Scenario
1 IRC — corrected-time order differs from elapsed-time order
2 PY — TCF derived from integer Portsmouth Yardstick number
3 IRC — DNF receives fleet-size penalty; finishers still scored on corrected time
4 IRC — competitor with no TCC is excluded from scoring; others scored normally
5 IRC — tie on corrected time triggers A8.1 averaging
6 IRC under A5.3 — DNF uses starters+1, DNC keeps entries+1
7 IRC under A5.3 with DNC from the starting area (DBSC SI A13.2)
8 IRC — penalty base counts rated boats only; unrated boats don't bloat penalties
9 IRC — corrected time rounded to whole seconds; sub-second float jitter must not reverse rank
10 IRC — RDG average excluding DNC up to the discard limit (handicap path)
11 IRC — mid-series TCC change via per-race rating override
12 IRC — series tie broken by RRS A8.1/A8.2 on the handicap path
13 IRC — additive scoring penalty (SCP) applies in a handicap fleet
14 VPRS — corrected-time order differs from finish order (spinnaker TCC)
15 VPRS — non-spinnaker TCCs, three races, one discard, A8 tie-break
16 VPRS — mid-series TCC change via per-race rating override
sailscoring.ie
